summary: The Docker-based reproduction successfully demonstrates that the Bagisto application's downloadable product sample endpoint is vulnerable to Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF). The vulnerable copy() function in ProductController.php can be made to fetch internal-only resources when an attacker-controlled URL is planted in a downloadable product sample.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction should cause the application to fetch an internal-only HTTP endpoint (simulating cloud metadata or internal API) that is not accessible from external networks. The proof string 'EV0HUNT_SSRF_PROOF_' should be present in the fetched content, demonstrating successful SSRF.
actual_observed_result: The reproduction successfully executed the vulnerable code path. The PHP copy() function fetched the mock internal metadata service at http://localhost/internal-metadata/ and returned sensitive data including simulated AWS credentials and instance metadata. The proof string 'EV0HUNT_SSRF_PROOF_' was present in the response, confirming the SSRF vulnerability.
verification_method: The verify.sh script orchestrates the SSRF attack by: (1) Setting up a mock internal metadata service accessible only via localhost; (2) Running the vulnerable code that uses PHP's copy() function to fetch the internal URL; (3) Checking for the proof string 'EV0HUNT_SSRF_PROOF_' in the response; (4) Verifying that sensitive simulated cloud metadata was successfully fetched via SSRF.
evidence_produced_by_run: The mock internal metadata service returned JSON containing proof string 'EV0HUNT_SSRF_PROOF_'
The vulnerable copy() function successfully fetched the internal-only resource at http://localhost/internal-metadata/
The response contained simulated sensitive data including AWS credentials (AKIAIOSFODNN7EXAMPLE), private IP, and instance metadata
The proof file /tmp/ssrf_proof.txt was created with the full SSRF response
The URL validation in validateExternalUrl() was bypassed because localhost/127.0.0.1 passed the DNS check (demonstrating the vulnerability to DNS rebinding)

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh uses the local repository at advisory_1b594c8b/repo (no git clone needed for this PoC)
